content.problemStatement:
You are given a rooted tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN). Node 111 is the root. You are also given integer sequences x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xNx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Nx1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äč and v1,v2,‚Ä¶,vNv_1, v_2, \ldots, v_Nv1‚Äč,v2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,vN‚Äč.

Let SSS be a subset of nodes. It is called valid if it is non-empty and the following conditions hold:


There is no pair of nodes (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that i,j‚ąąSi,j \in Si,j‚ąąS and iii is an ancestor of jjj.
The greatest common divisor of the values xix_ixi‚Äč for all nodes i‚ąąSi \in Si‚ąąS (let's denote it by GGG) is greater than 111.
Next, let's define the value of SSS as G‚čÖVG \cdot VG‚čÖV, where GGG is defined above and V=‚ąĎi‚ąąSviV = \sum_{i \in S}{v_i}V=‚ąĎi‚ąąS‚Äčvi‚Äč.

You need to find a valid subset of nodes with the maximum value.
constraints: the graph described on the input is a tree
1‚Č§xi,vi‚Č§100,0001 \le x_i, v_i \le 100,0001‚Č§xi‚Äč,vi‚Äč‚Č§100,000 for each valid iii
at least one valid subset exists
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 1,000,0001,000,0001,000,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the next N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that nodes uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers xix_ixi‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print three lines.


The first of these lines should contain two space-separated integers G‚čÖVG \cdot VG‚čÖV and GGG for your chosen subset.
The second line should contain a single integer KKK ‚Äē the size of your subset.
The third line should contain KKK space-separated integers ‚Äē the numbers of the nodes in this subset (in any order).
If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
